lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਗੁ ਆਸਾ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ਅਸਟਪਦੀਆ ਘਰੁ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: raag aasaa mahalaa 1 asattapadeea ghar 3 |
lines.english: Raag Aasaa, First Mehl, Ashtpadheeyaa, Third House:

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਹਲਾ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਹੋਦੀਆ ਹੁਣਿ ਬਹਣਿ ਨ ਮਿਲਨੑਿ ਹਦੂਰਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: mahalaa andar hodeea hun behan na milana hadoor |1|
lines.english: They lived in palatial mansions, but now, they cannot even sit near the palaces. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇ ਸਿਰ ਕਾਤੀ ਮੁੰਨੀਅਨੑਿ ਗਲ ਵਿਚਿ ਆਵੈ ਧੂੜਿ ॥
lines.translit: se sir kaatee muneeana gal vich aavai dhoorr |
lines.english: those heads were shaved with scissors, and their throats were choked with dust.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਸਿਰਿ ਸੋਹਨਿ ਪਟੀਆ ਮਾਂਗੀ ਪਾਇ ਸੰਧੂਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: jin sir sohan patteea maangee paae sandhoor |
lines.english: Those heads adorned with braided hair, with their parts painted with vermillion

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਦੇਸੁ ਬਾਬਾ ਆਦੇਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: aades baabaa aades |
lines.english: Hail to You, O Father Lord, Hail to You!

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਦਿ ਪੁਰਖ ਤੇਰਾ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਦੇਖਹਿ ਵੇਸ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aad purakh teraa ant na paaeaa kar kar dekheh ves |1| rahaau |
lines.english: O Primal Lord. Your limits are not known; You create, and create, and behold the scenes. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੀਡੋਲੀ ਚੜਿ ਆਈਆ ਦੰਦ ਖੰਡ ਕੀਤੇ ਰਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: heeddolee charr aaeea dand khandd keete raas |
lines.english: They came in palanquins, decorated with ivory;

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਦਹੁ ਸੀਆ ਵੀਆਹੀਆ ਲਾੜੇ ਸੋਹਨਿ ਪਾਸਿ ॥
lines.translit: jadahu seea veeaaheea laarre sohan paas |
lines.english: When they were married, their husbands looked so handsome beside them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਪਰਹੁ ਪਾਣੀ ਵਾਰੀਐ ਝਲੇ ਝਿਮਕਨਿ ਪਾਸਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: auparahu paanee vaareeai jhale jhimakan paas |2|
lines.english: water was sprinkled over their heads, and glittering fans were waved above them. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕੁ ਲਖੁ ਲਹਨੑਿ ਬਹਿਠੀਆ ਲਖੁ ਲਹਨੑਿ ਖੜੀਆ ॥
lines.translit: eik lakh lehana bahittheea lakh lehana kharreea |
lines.english: They were given hundreds of thousands of coins when they sat, and hundreds of thousands of coins when they stood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨੑ ਗਲਿ ਸਿਲਕਾ ਪਾਈਆ ਤੁਟਨੑਿ ਮੋਤਸਰੀਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: tina gal silakaa paaeea tuttana motasareea |3|
lines.english: But ropes were put around their necks, and their strings of pearls were broken. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਰੀ ਛੁਹਾਰੇ ਖਾਂਦੀਆ ਮਾਣਨੑਿ ਸੇਜੜੀਆ ॥
lines.translit: garee chhuhaare khaandeea maanana sejarreea |
lines.english: They ate coconuts and dates, and rested comfortably upon their beds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਨੁ ਜੋਬਨੁ ਦੁਇ ਵੈਰੀ ਹੋਏ ਜਿਨੑੀ ਰਖੇ ਰੰਗੁ ਲਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: dhan joban due vairee hoe jinaee rakhe rang laae |
lines.english: Their wealth and youthful beauty, which gave them so much pleasure, have now become their enemies.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਤਾ ਨੋ ਫੁਰਮਾਇਆ ਲੈ ਚਲੇ ਪਤਿ ਗਵਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: dootaa no furamaaeaa lai chale pat gavaae |
lines.english: The order was given to the soldiers, who dishonored them, and carried them away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਦੇ ਵਡਿਆਈ ਜੇ ਭਾਵੈ ਦੇਇ ਸਜਾਇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: je tis bhaavai de vaddiaaee je bhaavai dee sajaae |4|
lines.english: If it is pleasing to God's Will, He bestows greatness; if is pleases His Will, He bestows punishment. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗੋ ਦੇ ਜੇ ਚੇਤੀਐ ਤਾਂ ਕਾਇਤੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਸਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: ago de je cheteeai taan kaaeit milai sajaae |
lines.english: If someone focuses on the Lord beforehand, then why should he be punished?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਾਂ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਗਵਾਈਆ ਰੰਗਿ ਤਮਾਸੈ ਚਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: saahaan surat gavaaeea rang tamaasai chaae |
lines.english: The kings had lost their higher consciousness, reveling in pleasure and sensuality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਬਰਵਾਣੀ ਫਿਰਿ ਗਈ ਕੁਇਰੁ ਨ ਰੋਟੀ ਖਾਇ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: baabaravaanee fir gee kueir na rottee khaae |5|
lines.english: Since Baabar's rule has been proclaimed, even the princes have no food to eat. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਉਕੇ ਵਿਣੁ ਹਿੰਦਵਾਣੀਆ ਕਿਉ ਟਿਕੇ ਕਢਹਿ ਨਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: chauke vin hindavaaneea kiau ttike kadteh naae |
lines.english: Without their sacred squares, how shall the Hindu women bathe and apply the frontal marks to their foreheads?

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮੁ ਨ ਕਬਹੂ ਚੇਤਿਓ ਹੁਣਿ ਕਹਣਿ ਨ ਮਿਲੈ ਖੁਦਾਇ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: raam na kabahoo chetio hun kehan na milai khudaae |6|
lines.english: They never remembered their Lord as Raam, and now they cannot even chant Khudaa-i||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਨਾ ਵਖਤ ਖੁਆਈਅਹਿ ਇਕਨੑਾ ਪੂਜਾ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: eikanaa vakhat khuaaeeeh ikanaa poojaa jaae |
lines.english: The Muslims have lost their five times of daily prayer, and the Hindus have lost their worship as well.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਘਰਿ ਆਵਹਿ ਆਪਣੈ ਇਕਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਪੁਛਹਿ ਸੁਖ ॥
lines.translit: eik ghar aaveh aapanai ik mil mil puchheh sukh |
lines.english: Some have returned to their homes, and meeting their relatives, they ask about their safety.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਨੑਾ ਏਹੋ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਬਹਿ ਬਹਿ ਰੋਵਹਿ ਦੁਖ ॥
lines.translit: eikanaa eho likhiaa beh beh roveh dukh |
lines.english: For some, it is pre-ordained that they shall sit and cry out in pain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਸੋ ਥੀਐ ਨਾਨਕ ਕਿਆ ਮਾਨੁਖ ॥੭॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: jo tis bhaavai so theeai naanak kiaa maanukh |7|11|
lines.english: Whatever pleases Him, comes to pass. O Nanak, what is the fate of mankind? ||7||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Aasaa, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਾ ਸੁ ਖੇਲ ਤਬੇਲਾ ਘੋੜੇ ਕਹਾ ਭੇਰੀ ਸਹਨਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: kahaa su khel tabelaa ghorre kahaa bheree sahanaaee |
lines.english: Where are the games, the stables, the horses? Where are the drums and the bugles?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਾ ਸੁ ਤੇਗਬੰਦ ਗਾਡੇਰੜਿ ਕਹਾ ਸੁ ਲਾਲ ਕਵਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: kahaa su tegaband gaadderarr kahaa su laal kavaaee |
lines.english: Where are the sword-belts and chariots? Where are those scarlet uniforms?

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕ ਘੜੀ ਮਹਿ ਥਾਪਿ ਉਥਾਪੇ ਜਰੁ ਵੰਡਿ ਦੇਵੈ ਭਾਂਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: ek gharree meh thaap uthaape jar vandd devai bhaanee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: In an instant, You establish and disestablish. You distribute wealth as it pleases You. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਾ ਸੁ ਆਰਸੀਆ ਮੁਹ ਬੰਕੇ ਐਥੈ ਦਿਸਹਿ ਨਾਹੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: kahaa su aaraseea muh banke aithai diseh naahee |1|
lines.english: Where are the rings and the beautiful faces? They are no longer to be seen here. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਜਗੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਤੂ ਗੋਸਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: eihu jag teraa too gosaaee |
lines.english: This world is Yours; You are the Lord of the Universe.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਾਂ ਸੁ ਘਰ ਦਰ ਮੰਡਪ ਮਹਲਾ ਕਹਾ ਸੁ ਬੰਕ ਸਰਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: kahaan su ghar dar manddap mahalaa kahaa su bank saraaee |
lines.english: Where are the houses, the gates, the hotels and palaces? Where are those beautiful way-stations?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਾ ਸੁ ਪਾਨ ਤੰਬੋਲੀ ਹਰਮਾ ਹੋਈਆ ਛਾਈ ਮਾਈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: kahaa su paan tanbolee haramaa hoeea chhaaee maaee |2|
lines.english: Where are those betel leaves, their sellers, and the haremees? They have vanished like shadows. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਾਂ ਸੁ ਸੇਜ ਸੁਖਾਲੀ ਕਾਮਣਿ ਜਿਸੁ ਵੇਖਿ ਨੀਦ ਨ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: kahaan su sej sukhaalee kaaman jis vekh need na paaee |
lines.english: Where are those beautiful women, reclining on their beds, whose beauty would not allow one to sleep?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਪਾ ਬਾਝਹੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਨਾਹੀ ਮੁਇਆ ਸਾਥਿ ਨ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: paapaa baajhahu hovai naahee mueaa saath na jaaee |
lines.english: It was not gathered without sin, and it does not go along with the dead.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸੁ ਜਰ ਕਾਰਣਿ ਘਣੀ ਵਿਗੁਤੀ ਇਨਿ ਜਰ ਘਣੀ ਖੁਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: eis jar kaaran ghanee vigutee in jar ghanee khuaaee |
lines.english: For the sake of this wealth, so many were ruined; because of this wealth, so many have been disgraced.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋਟੀ ਹੂ ਪੀਰ ਵਰਜਿ ਰਹਾਏ ਜਾ ਮੀਰੁ ਸੁਣਿਆ ਧਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: kottee hoo peer varaj rahaae jaa meer suniaa dhaaeaa |
lines.english: Millions of religious leaders failed to halt the invader, when they heard of the Emperor's invasion.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਆਪਿ ਖੁਆਏ ਕਰਤਾ ਖੁਸਿ ਲਏ ਚੰਗਿਆਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jis no aap khuaae karataa khus le changiaaee |3|
lines.english: Those, whom the Creator Lord would destroy - first He strips them of virtue. ||3||
